lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਜੇ ਮੈ ਹੋਦਾ ਵਾਰਿਆ ਮਿਤਾ ਆਇੜਿਆਂ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa je mai hodaa vaariaa mitaa aaeirriaan |
lines.english: Fareed, if I had been there when my friend came, I would have made myself a sacrifice to him.

lines.gurmukhi: ਧਿਗੁ ਤਿਨੑਾ ਦਾ ਜੀਵਿਆ ਜਿਨਾ ਵਿਡਾਣੀ ਆਸ ॥੨੧॥
lines.translit: dhig tinaa daa jeeviaa jinaa viddaanee aas |21|
lines.english: Cursed are the lives of those who place their hopes in others. ||21||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਲੋੜੈ ਦਾਖ ਬਿਜਉਰੀਆਂ ਕਿਕਰਿ ਬੀਜੈ ਜਟੁ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa lorrai daakh bijaureean kikar beejai jatt |
lines.english: Fareed, the farmer plants acacia trees, and wishes for grapes.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹੇੜਾ ਜਲੈ ਮਜੀਠ ਜਿਉ ਉਪਰਿ ਅੰਗਾਰਾ ॥੨੨॥
lines.translit: herraa jalai majeetth jiau upar angaaraa |22|
lines.english: Now my flesh is burning red on the hot coals. ||22||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹੰਢੈ ਉਂਨ ਕਤਾਇਦਾ ਪੈਧਾ ਲੋੜੈ ਪਟੁ ॥੨੩॥
lines.translit: handtai un kataaeidaa paidhaa lorrai patt |23|
lines.english: He is spinning wool, but he wishes to wear silk. ||23||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਗਲੀਏ ਚਿਕੜੁ ਦੂਰਿ ਘਰੁ ਨਾਲਿ ਪਿਆਰੇ ਨੇਹੁ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa galee chikarr door ghar naal piaare nehu |
lines.english: Fareed, the path is muddy, and the house of my Beloved is so far away.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾਇ ਮਿਲਾ ਤਿਨਾ ਸਜਣਾ ਤੁਟਉ ਨਾਹੀ ਨੇਹੁ ॥੨੫॥
lines.translit: jaae milaa tinaa sajanaa tuttau naahee nehu |25|
lines.english: I am going out to meet my Friend, so that my heart will not be broken. ||25||

lines.gurmukhi: ਭਿਜਉ ਸਿਜਉ ਕੰਬਲੀ ਅਲਹ ਵਰਸਉ ਮੇਹੁ ॥
lines.translit: bhijau sijau kanbalee alah varsau mehu |
lines.english: My blanket is soaked, drenched with the downpour of the Lord's Rain.

lines.gurmukhi: ਚਲਾ ਤ ਭਿਜੈ ਕੰਬਲੀ ਰਹਾਂ ਤ ਤੁਟੈ ਨੇਹੁ ॥੨੪॥
lines.translit: chalaa ta bhijai kanbalee rahaan ta tuttai nehu |24|
lines.english: If I go out, my blanket will get soaked, but if I remain at home, then my heart will be broken. ||24||

lines.gurmukhi: ਗਹਿਲਾ ਰੂਹੁ ਨ ਜਾਣਈ ਸਿਰੁ ਭੀ ਮਿਟੀ ਖਾਇ ॥੨੬॥
lines.translit: gahilaa roohu na jaanee sir bhee mittee khaae |26|
lines.english: My thoughtless self did not realize that one day, dust will consume my head as well. ||26||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਮੈ ਭੋਲਾਵਾ ਪਗ ਦਾ ਮਤੁ ਮੈਲੀ ਹੋਇ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa mai bholaavaa pag daa mat mailee hoe jaae |
lines.english: Fareed, I was worried that my turban might become dirty.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਭੇ ਵਸਤੂ ਮਿਠੀਆਂ ਰਬ ਨ ਪੁਜਨਿ ਤੁਧੁ ॥੨੭॥
lines.translit: sabhe vasatoo mittheean rab na pujan tudh |27|
lines.english: - all these things are sweet, but they are not equal to You. ||27||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਸਕਰ ਖੰਡੁ ਨਿਵਾਤ ਗੁੜੁ ਮਾਖਿਓੁ ਮਾਂਝਾ ਦੁਧੁ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa sakar khandd nivaat gurr maakhio maanjhaa dudh |
lines.english: Fareed: sugar cane, candy, sugar, molasses, honey and buffalo's milk

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨਾ ਖਾਧੀ ਚੋਪੜੀ ਘਣੇ ਸਹਨਿਗੇ ਦੁਖ ॥੨੮॥
lines.translit: jinaa khaadhee choparree ghane sahanige dukh |28|
lines.english: Those who eat buttered bread, will suffer in terrible pain. ||28||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਰੋਟੀ ਮੇਰੀ ਕਾਠ ਕੀ ਲਾਵਣੁ ਮੇਰੀ ਭੁਖ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa rottee meree kaatth kee laavan meree bhukh |
lines.english: Fareed, my bread is made of wood, and hunger is my appetizer.

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਦੇਖਿ ਪਰਾਈ ਚੋਪੜੀ ਨਾ ਤਰਸਾਏ ਜੀਉ ॥੨੯॥
lines.translit: fareedaa dekh paraaee choparree naa tarasaae jeeo |29|
lines.english: Fareed, if you see someone else's buttered bread, do not envy him for it. ||29||

lines.gurmukhi: ਰੁਖੀ ਸੁਖੀ ਖਾਇ ਕੈ ਠੰਢਾ ਪਾਣੀ ਪੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: rukhee sukhee khaae kai tthandtaa paanee peeo |
lines.english: Eat dry bread, and drink cold water.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਜੁ ਨ ਸੁਤੀ ਕੰਤ ਸਿਉ ਅੰਗੁ ਮੁੜੇ ਮੁੜਿ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: aj na sutee kant siau ang murre murr jaae |
lines.english: This night, I did not sleep with my Husband Lord, and now my body is suffering in pain.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾਇ ਪੁਛਹੁ ਡੋਹਾਗਣੀ ਤੁਮ ਕਿਉ ਰੈਣਿ ਵਿਹਾਇ ॥੩੦॥
lines.translit: jaae puchhahu ddohaaganee tum kiau rain vihaae |30|
lines.english: Go and ask the deserted bride, how she passes her night. ||30||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਿਰੁ ਵਾਤੜੀ ਨ ਪੁਛਈ ਧਨ ਸੋਹਾਗਣਿ ਨਾਉ ॥੩੧॥
lines.translit: pir vaatarree na puchhee dhan sohaagan naau |31|
lines.english: Her Husband Lord does not care for her; what sort of a blessed, happy soul-bride is she? ||31||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਹੁਰੈ ਢੋਈ ਨਾ ਲਹੈ ਪੇਈਐ ਨਾਹੀ ਥਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: saahurai dtoee naa lahai peeeai naahee thaau |
lines.english: She finds no place of rest in her father-in-law's home, and no place in her parents' home either.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਸੋ ਸੋਹਾਗਣੀ ਜੁ ਭਾਵੈ ਬੇਪਰਵਾਹ ॥੩੨॥
lines.translit: naanak so sohaaganee ju bhaavai beparavaah |32|
lines.english: O Nanak, she is the happy soul-bride, who is pleasing to her Carefree Lord. ||32||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਹੁਰੈ ਪੇਈਐ ਕੰਤ ਕੀ ਕੰਤੁ ਅਗੰਮੁ ਅਥਾਹੁ ॥
lines.translit: saahurai peeeai kant kee kant agam athaahu |
lines.english: In her father-in-law's home hereafter, and in her parents' home in this world, she belongs to her Husband Lord. Her Husband is Inaccessible and Unfathomable.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਤੀ ਧੋਤੀ ਸੰਬਹੀ ਸੁਤੀ ਆਇ ਨਚਿੰਦੁ ॥
lines.translit: naatee dhotee sanbahee sutee aae nachind |
lines.english: Bathing, washing and decorating herself, she comes and sleeps without anxiety.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋਬਨ ਜਾਂਦੇ ਨਾ ਡਰਾਂ ਜੇ ਸਹ ਪ੍ਰੀਤਿ ਨ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: joban jaande naa ddaraan je seh preet na jaae |
lines.english: I am not afraid of losing my youth, as long as I do not lose the Love of my Husband Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਰਹੀ ਸੁ ਬੇੜੀ ਹਿੰਙੁ ਦੀ ਗਈ ਕਥੂਰੀ ਗੰਧੁ ॥੩੩॥
lines.translit: fareedaa rahee su berree hing dee gee kathooree gandh |33|
lines.english: Fareed, she still smells like asafoetida; the fragrance of musk is gone. ||33||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਕਿਤਂ‍ੀ ਜੋਬਨ ਪ੍ਰੀਤਿ ਬਿਨੁ ਸੁਕਿ ਗਏ ਕੁਮਲਾਇ ॥੩੪॥
lines.translit: fareedaa kitanee joban preet bin suk ge kumalaae |34|
lines.english: Fareed, so many youths, without His Love, have dried up and withered away. ||34||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਚਿੰਤ ਖਟੋਲਾ ਵਾਣੁ ਦੁਖੁ ਬਿਰਹਿ ਵਿਛਾਵਣ ਲੇਫੁ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa chint khattolaa vaan dukh bireh vichhaavan lef |
lines.english: Fareed, anxiety is my bed, pain is my mattress, and the pain of separation is my blanket and quilt.

lines.gurmukhi: ਏਹੁ ਹਮਾਰਾ ਜੀਵਣਾ ਤੂ ਸਾਹਿਬ ਸਚੇ ਵੇਖੁ ॥੩੫॥
lines.translit: ehu hamaaraa jeevanaa too saahib sache vekh |35|
lines.english: Behold, this is my life, O my True Lord and Master. ||35||

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਰਹਾ ਬਿਰਹਾ ਆਖੀਐ ਬਿਰਹਾ ਤੂ ਸੁਲਤਾਨੁ ॥
lines.translit: birahaa birahaa aakheeai birahaa too sulataan |
lines.english: Many talk of the pain and suffering of separation; O pain, you are the ruler of all.

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਜਿਤੁ ਤਨਿ ਬਿਰਹੁ ਨ ਊਪਜੈ ਸੋ ਤਨੁ ਜਾਣੁ ਮਸਾਨੁ ॥੩੬॥
lines.translit: fareedaa jit tan birahu na aoopajai so tan jaan masaan |36|
lines.english: Fareed, that body, within which love of the Lord does not well up - look upon that body as a cremation ground. ||36||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਏ ਵਿਸੁ ਗੰਦਲਾ ਧਰੀਆਂ ਖੰਡੁ ਲਿਵਾੜਿ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa e vis gandalaa dhareean khandd livaarr |
lines.english: Fareed, these are poisonous sprouts coated with sugar.

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਚਾਰਿ ਗਵਾਇਆ ਹੰਢਿ ਕੈ ਚਾਰਿ ਗਵਾਇਆ ਸੰਮਿ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa chaar gavaaeaa handt kai chaar gavaaeaa sam |
lines.english: Fareed, the hours of the day are lost wandering around, and the hours of the night are lost in sleep.

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਕਿ ਰਾਹੇਦੇ ਰਹਿ ਗਏ ਇਕਿ ਰਾਧੀ ਗਏ ਉਜਾੜਿ ॥੩੭॥
lines.translit: eik raahede reh ge ik raadhee ge ujaarr |37|
lines.english: Some die planting them, and some are ruined, harvesting and enjoying them. ||37||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਦਰਿ ਦਰਵਾਜੈ ਜਾਇ ਕੈ ਕਿਉ ਡਿਠੋ ਘੜੀਆਲੁ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa dar daravaajai jaae kai kiau ddittho gharreeaal |
lines.english: Fareed, you have gone to the Lord's Door. Have you seen the gong there?

lines.gurmukhi: ਲੇਖਾ ਰਬੁ ਮੰਗੇਸੀਆ ਤੂ ਆਂਹੋ ਕੇਰ੍ਹੇ ਕੰਮਿ ॥੩੮॥
lines.translit: lekhaa rab mangeseea too aanho kerhe kam |38|
lines.english: God will call for your account, and ask you why you came into this world. ||38||

lines.gurmukhi: ਏਹੁ ਨਿਦੋਸਾਂ ਮਾਰੀਐ ਹਮ ਦੋਸਾਂ ਦਾ ਕਿਆ ਹਾਲੁ ॥੩੯॥
lines.translit: ehu nidosaan maareeai ham dosaan daa kiaa haal |39|
lines.english: This blameless object is being beaten - imagine what is in store for us sinners! ||39||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੋ ਹੇੜਾ ਘੜੀਆਲ ਜਿਉ ਡੁਖੀ ਰੈਣਿ ਵਿਹਾਇ ॥੪੦॥
lines.translit: so herraa gharreeaal jiau ddukhee rain vihaae |40|
lines.english: This beautiful body is like the gong; it passes the night in pain. ||40||

lines.gurmukhi: ਘੜੀਏ ਘੜੀਏ ਮਾਰੀਐ ਪਹਰੀ ਲਹੈ ਸਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: gharree gharree maareeai paharee lahai sajaae |
lines.english: Each and every hour, it is beaten; it is punished every day.
